cases.description: Happy path: single Python traceback showing AttributeError from unguarded None return
cases.prompt:
Here are the logs from our CI run. Fix the bug and write a test to prevent it coming back.

```
2026-04-04 09:12:33 ERROR cart.py:87 in process_checkout
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'charge_id'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "cart.py", line 87, in process_checkout
    receipt = payment_gateway.charge(order.total)
    return receipt.charge_id
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'charge_id'
```
assertions: skill enters plan mode (EnterPlanMode called) before any file edit
plan contains a root cause statement identifying that payment_gateway.charge() can return None
a pytest test function is written before the code fix
test name contains 'none' or 'returns_none' or 'charge_returns_none'
test uses unittest.mock.patch or MagicMock to mock payment_gateway
test mock is configured to return None (the failure condition)
fix is a guard or None-check at the call site or a raised exception, not a refactor
pytest is run after the fix and result is confirmed green

cases.description: Cascading failure: 40+ errors across 5 modules all caused by a single DB connection failure
cases.prompt:
Production logs from last night. Multiple modules failing, need this fixed.

```
2026-04-04 02:01:05 ERROR orders/repository.py:34 in get_order
  sqlalchemy.exc.OperationalError: (psycopg2.OperationalError) could not connect to server
2026-04-04 02:01:05 ERROR orders/service.py:61 in fetch_pending_orders
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'status'
2026-04-04 02:01:05 ERROR notifications/sender.py:22 in notify_user
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'email'
2026-04-04 02:01:05 ERROR billing/invoicer.py:88 in generate_invoice
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'total'
2026-04-04 02:01:05 ERROR api/orders_endpoint.py:15 in list_orders
  TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
```
assertions: skill clusters the 5 errors into 1 root-cause cluster (not 5 separate bugs)
root cause identified as orders/repository.py DB connection failure, not the downstream AttributeErrors
plan presents the cascade explanation before proposing fixes
fix targets the DB connection handling (retry logic, circuit breaker, or error propagation), not the downstream None checks
regression test mocks the DB session to raise OperationalError
plan mode entered before any file edits

cases.description: Negative trigger: user asks for a refactor with no logs — skill should NOT activate
cases.prompt: Can you refactor the orders module to use a repository pattern? The current code mixes DB calls and business logic.
assertions: skill does NOT activate
no Phase 1 triage is run
no test is written
no EnterPlanMode call is made in response to this request
response addresses the refactor request directly without running the fix-from-logs workflow

cases.description: Edge case: intermittent CI failure suggesting a time-dependent or ordering bug
cases.prompt:
This test fails in CI about 30% of the time but never locally. Here's the CI log:

```
FAILED tests/test_scheduler.py::test_task_runs_at_scheduled_time
AssertionError: assert datetime(2026, 4, 4, 9, 0, 1) == datetime(2026, 4, 4, 9, 0, 0)
  Actual:   2026-04-04 09:00:01
  Expected: 2026-04-04 09:00:00
```
assertions: skill identifies this as a time-dependent / flaky test (intermittent failure pattern)
root cause hypothesis mentions datetime.now() or time.time() non-determinism
fix recommendation includes freezegun or a time-injection approach
regression test uses freezegun.freeze_time decorator or patches datetime
plan mode entered before any changes
